comments.author: Avi
comments.body: When I got back to the hotel last night, I noticed these door knobs, and they reminded me of the bamboo band detail we discussed yesterday. I wanted to share this image purely as a reference, not something to replicate exactly. What I think is useful here is how the longer portion softens and then opens up into the stone area in a clean, tailored way. While this example feels a bit too straight compared to what we discussed, it does a nice job showing the tapered transition from the stone into the metal, which aligns with the direction we were talking about. Sharing this mainly to help visualize how that long element can feel lighter and more refined as it meets the stone.

We have a running style RX140080C that has the micro prongs setting around the MQ center.  It is the same concept but in emerald cut center stone. Please call me if you have any questions.
The halo in the drawing does not match the image. The image shows a finer, smaller halo but the halo in the drawing is too wide.

We will not be moving forward with the 2 1/2 CT version. After reviewing with Baiju these are the notes for the 3 CT: Use +2-2.5 x 24 stones for the halo (need to keep the halo delicate so it does not overpower the look of the emerald, can space out if needed) Use +5-5.5 x 14 stones for the band (don't need the diamond run down to go too far down, keep at 3-9o'clock position max) Center emerald stone will be approx. 2.53CT.
The BOM has been approved. Is there any way to lower the center from 7mm to 6.6? Make sure prongs are not rough (JW)
okay to proceed.  pls rush and ship it out by this weekend.  we have a few meetings coming up

Question for working the designs. Need some RnD from your CAD team.For P246442LY, can the cad designer look at the 3dm model and add a fingerprint zone on the front a 2nd zone on the back. In the future we could do pieces with multiple finger print zones.How to mark them: Draw boundary curves on the surface where each fingerprint zone starts and ends Split the surface along those curves Name each fingerprint-eligible piece: FP_ZONE_1, FP_ZONE_2, FP_ZONE_3, etc. Leave everything else as isThink of it like cutting a pizza. You're slicing the existing surface into pieces. Nothing new is added on top, no floating layers, no extra geometry. The pieces are still the original surface, just divided and labeled. I need to know how long it takes to add these zones.Could you also do PDG040, in this style each cutout on the front should be a different finger print zone (5 total).
